title: galium palustre var. constrictum
intro: Galium palustre var. constrictum, also known as short-stemmed marsh bedstraw, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the bedstraw family (Galium). This plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Galium palustre var. constrictum include its ability to reduce pain and inflammation, as well as its antiseptic properties, which can help prevent infection and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of Galium palustre var. constrictum typically involve drying and infusing the plant's leaves and stems in hot water to create a tea or infusion that can be used to treat a variety of health issues.
